benchmark_report.title: SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKING REPORT
benchmark_report.subtitle: Scientific Analysis with Brutal Honesty
benchmark_report.methodology: Random selection, statistical analysis, CLAUDE.md rule compliance

executive_summary.savage_verdict: 60% of analyzed commands are RULE VIOLATORS that would make Martin Fowler weep
key_findings: 80% complexity score violations - These commands have more layers than a wedding cake
20% have failure rates that would make Vegas jealous
Average token cost of 2,878 - Your wallet called, it's filing for bankruptcy
One command has 130 variable substitutions - That's not templating, that's therapy
executive_summary.biggest_offender: adhd-context-switch.md with failure risk score of 7/10 - A command so broken it needs its own therapist

complexity_score.savage_comment: Mean of 4.2/5 - That's not 'intelligent', that's cognitive overload with a PhD

failure_risk_score.savage_comment: σ=2.19 - This variability is wider than the Grand Canyon of disappointment

token_cost.savage_comment: Average cost of 2,878 tokens - That's $0.86 per execution. Hope your commands print money

adhd-context-switch.md.savage_analysis: This command has 130 variable substitutions and 4 user prompts. It's not a command, it's a Mad Libs book that happens to execute code. Failure rate higher than a skydiving instructor with trust issues.
adhd-context-switch.md.improvement_urgency: CRITICAL - Refactor immediately or face digital karma

intelligent-code-enhancer.md.savage_analysis: Max complexity score with 48 thinking blocks. This isn't intelligent enhancement, it's the AI equivalent of a Rube Goldberg machine. It works, but at what cost to humanity?

adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md.savage_analysis: Most expensive at 3,370 tokens but keeps complexity in check. Like a Rolls Royce - it works beautifully but your accountant will file for divorce.

adaptive-complexity-router.md.savage_analysis: A command designed to manage complexity that itself violates complexity rules. The irony is thicker than medieval armor. 60 thinking blocks to route thinking - that's not adaptation, that's inception.

ultrathink.md.savage_analysis: Shortest line count but max complexity. It's like a haiku written by Stephen Hawking - dense, profound, and completely over-engineered for most use cases.

claude_md_compliance.savage_verdict: More rule violations than a speed trap on a highway

complexity_rule.evidence: Complexity scores of 5/5 - CLAUDE.md clearly states <5
complexity_rule.punishment_suggestion: Force authors to implement FizzBuzz with their own commands

simplicity_principle.punishment_suggestion: Make them explain their code to a rubber duck

statistical_significance.trend_analysis: Complexity creep detected - newer commands tend toward over-engineering

cost_benefit_analysis.roi_assessment: Questionable - These commands solve real problems but at enterprise software prices

immediate_actions: EMERGENCY: Refactor adhd-context-switch.md - it's a ticking time bomb
URGENT: Simplify the 'intelligent' and 'adaptive' commands - they're neither
REQUIRED: Add complexity budgets to prevent future violations
strategic_improvements: Implement CLAUDE.md complexity enforcement in CI/CD
Create 'Command Diet' program to reduce bloat
Establish token budgets per command type
Add automated testing for actual executability
philosophical_guidance: Remember: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication
A working simple solution beats an elegant broken one
If it takes 500 lines to explain, it's probably wrong

hall_of_shame.most_failure_prone: adhd-context-switch.md (7/10) - Like a house of cards in a tornado
hall_of_shame.most_expensive: adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.md (3,370 tokens) - Premium pricing for premium confusion
hall_of_shame.most_ironic: adaptive-complexity-router.md - Managing complexity with maximum complexity

scientific_conclusion.evidence: 60% rule violation rate with statistical significance
scientific_conclusion.final_savage_verdict: These commands have more problems than a calculus textbook written by a caffeinated philosopher. They work, but at the cost of your sanity, your tokens, and probably your will to live. Time for a reality check, a complexity diet, and maybe a group therapy session for over-engineered software.
